bullets.text: Iran conflict elevates tail risks to global growth via shipping disruptions; U.S.-backed Hormuz protection coalition not yet formed, keeping markets on edge.
Oil prices jump as Hormuz security doubts persist; higher energy costs could quickly feed inflation and recession risks if sustained.
Moody’s warns recession becomes hard to avoid if oil remains elevated for weeks, highlighting energy-driven downside risk to consumer demand and margins.
Despite geopolitical shock, U.S. equities remain relatively resilient; positioning suggests investors still expect contained economic spillovers or policy backstops.
Dollar strengthens amid Iran-related inflation fears and risk-off demand, reinforcing the dollar’s safe-haven role even when the shock is oil-price driven.
Nvidia projects up to $1T in Blackwell and Vera Rubin orders through 2027, underscoring hyperscaler capex strength and AI infrastructure demand concentration.
Nvidia launches Vera CPU tailored for agentic AI, signaling vertical integration beyond GPUs as AI workloads shift toward full-stack, security, and efficiency constraints.
Prominent investors warn of an AI bubble and valuation reset; private equity also questions software marks, implying potential write-down and funding tightening risk.
SEC reportedly preparing to scrap quarterly reporting (shift to semiannual), potentially reducing short-termism but lowering disclosure frequency for public-market investors.
U.S. weighs conditioning HIV aid to Zambia on greater minerals access, tying health assistance to critical-minerals strategy and raising ethical and diplomatic risks.
Cuba signals readiness for outside investment while U.S. pressure targets leadership change during negotiations—creating high political risk for any foreign capital.
Partial U.S. shutdown leaves TSA unpaid, lengthening airport security lines and increasing travel friction—an incremental drag on services activity and consumer sentiment.
New biotech efforts aim for oral GLP-1 obesity drugs potentially competitive with current injectables, raising stakes in weight-loss pharmacology and payer cost dynamics.
Study links common pesticide exposure to more than doubled Parkinson’s risk, intensifying regulatory and liability pressure around agricultural chemicals.
Researchers report systemic cancer regression after injecting a single tumor, supporting next-generation immunotherapy concepts that could broaden efficacy beyond treated sites.
